_comment: Compaction-fidelity fixtures. Each case carries load-bearing facts as DISTINCTIVE, unparaphrasable tokens (codenames, amounts, IDs, versions) stated in `turns`. The runner lays these turns in the OLD head (which gets summarized away), pads with filler, then sends the transcript to /api/eval/compact with force=true. Score = how many `facts` survive verbatim in the compacted output. Categories mirror the COMPACTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT extraction targets (constraints / user-facts / decisions / outstanding-asks).

turns.user: Hard rule for this whole project: only ever deploy to the STAGING-7f3a environment. Never deploy to production under any circumstance.
turns.assistant: Understood. I'll restrict every deploy to STAGING-7f3a and never touch production.

turns.user: Keep total monthly cloud spend under $4,217. If a change would push us over that, stop and ask me first.
turns.assistant: Got it — I'll treat $4,217 as the hard monthly ceiling and check with you before exceeding it.

turns.user: For context about me: my internal codename on the team is Falcon-92 and I work in timezone UTC+5:45, so schedule things accordingly.
turns.assistant: Noted — you're Falcon-92, working in UTC+5:45. I'll factor that into any scheduling.

turns.user: One important personal detail to remember: I'm allergic to penicillin, so never suggest it if we ever discuss the first-aid app's content.
turns.assistant: I'll remember you're allergic to penicillin and avoid recommending it anywhere.

turns.user: Decision locked in from our review: we're going with PostgreSQL 16.2 for the datastore, not Mongo. Build everything around that.
turns.assistant: Locked in — PostgreSQL 16.2 is the datastore, Mongo is out. I'll design around Postgres 16.2.

turns.user: We agreed to pin the partner API to version v3.8.1 exactly — do not auto-upgrade past it, their v4 breaks our auth.
turns.assistant: Pinned: partner API stays at v3.8.1, no upgrades past it.

turns.user: Don't let me forget: commit a1b2c3d still needs a security review before we can ship. It's blocking the release.
turns.assistant: I'll keep that open — commit a1b2c3d needs a security review before release; it's a blocker.

turns.user: Still outstanding on the finance side: we're waiting on invoice #INV-90852 to be paid before the vendor unblocks us.
turns.assistant: Tracked — invoice #INV-90852 is unpaid and blocking the vendor; I'll surface it.
